summary: Persist a requirement's authored prose: rename `means` to the corpus noun `prose` SDK-and-engine-wide, emit it in the requirement's lock row, and retire the phantom never-interprets quote

per.section: requirement — a shipped kind, not a primitive

edit.description: Rename `Requirement.means: string` (lines 152-158) to `prose` — contract.md's template noun ("prose — the authored intent the requirement exists to carry; never interpreted"). Opening this file fires the riding exit clause on its 12 pre-0002 spec-path doc-comment citations (lines 5,7,17,34,38,48,57,89,124,142,149,160): cut them on contact, never annotate.
requirementRows() (lines 302-330) emits the prose in each row — today an authored `means` verifiably reaches neither the lock nor explain (zero `means` reads anywhere in this file).

Rename `Requirement.means: Option<String>` (line 71) to `prose`; the line-60 doc comment states the same phantom never-interprets ruling — restate it as contract.md's actual bar: carried verbatim, never interpreted.
RequirementRow (~line 1204) gains the prose column, `#[serde(default)]` like its optional siblings.

requirement_from_row (~line 1063) carries the prose from the row instead of defaulting `means: None`; retire the doc comment quoting the phantom ruling "`temper` never interprets `means`" — zero hits in specs/; contract.md says prose is *carried*, never interpreted — a statement about carrying it, not license to drop it.
Rename ripple only: explain's requirement narration already surfaces the field (lines 272, 369, 437) — with persistence fixed it starts carrying real words.
Rename ripple: `means` fixture/comment hits (lines 474, 916-920).
Rename ripple: `means` fixture hit (line 1336).
Rename ripple: module docs, the `requirement()` test builder, and the means-less-requirement tests (lines 5-28, 79, 161-167, 259-261).
Rename ripple: fixture at line 233.
Rename ripple: lock-snapshot fixture at line 640 — the emitted row key changes with the rename.
Rename ripple (line 47), plus a new assert: explain narrates an authored prose verbatim.

Rename ripple in fixtures. Opening this file fires the riding exit clause on the stale `effective_governs` doc comment (lines 336-338 — e5daf1d renamed the symbol to `overlay_builtin_kind`): fix on contact.
New assert: a requirement's prose round-trips SDK emit -> lock row -> engine compose verbatim.
Rename ripple in fixtures (lines 134, 302-303), plus assert requirementRows carries the prose.
Rename ripple in fixtures (lines 41, 60, 99).

tests.asserts: a requirement's prose round-trips SDK emit -> lock row -> engine compose verbatim
explain narrates a requirement's authored prose
requirementRows emits the prose in the declaration row

acceptance: An authored requirement's prose reaches the committed lock and explain's narration verbatim; `means` and the phantom never-interprets quote grep to zero as symbols across src/ and sdk/src/; all gates green.
notes: Scoped at f600965. Inbox note observed 9c3b1c1; no code commit since touches these surfaces. The template's other slots already ship (identity/verified_by/clauses in RequirementRow; read.rs already narrates the field) — the verified gap is persistence plus the phantom quote (main.rs ~1063, compose.rs:60). Opening contract.ts and tests/coverage.rs fires their riding exit-clause debts — cut on contact. If 0019's derivation demands a fuller kind reification, that is a further slice.

summary: Re-fetch the skill kind's doc sources, diff skillDefaultContract's clauses against them, fix any drifted clause, restamp every stale cite (skill 07-01; rule/memory 07-01/02, same walk)

edit.description: Every skillDefaultContract clause cite (lines 182-252) still reads retrieved 2026-07-01; the 07-07 fetch that shipped command/agent demonstrably changed the neighboring kinds and never re-checked skill's. Re-fetch code.claude.com/docs/en/skills + agentskills.io/specification, diff each clause, fix drift, restamp the retrieved dates. The same walk covers the rule/memory clause cites from 07-01/07-02 (lines ~319-398) — one ritual, one file. commandDefaultContract derives from skill's by filter: verify it inherits every fix.

The embedded built-in lock carries the clause rows (cites included) — re-derive it from the updated module if any clause changed, so tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs's byte-compare stays green; never hand-transcribe (distribution.md).
Existing clause assertions may pin the old cites or guidance — reconcile with whatever the fresh fetch changed.

tests.asserts: the embedded built-in lock re-derives from the updated module and byte-compares (existing gate; regenerated lock lands in the same commit)

acceptance: No skillDefaultContract cite predates the fetch that validated it; any clause the current docs contradict is fixed in the same commit; built-in lock frozen gate green.
notes: Scoped at f600965. Inbox note observed 9c3b1c1; no commit since touches skill's citations (706139a was a pure rename). Requires a live doc fetch at build time — the same fetch-and-cite pattern efd6caa/81b6ab4 ran on 07-07. External-facts bar (collaboration rule): verify before encoding — never restamp a date without reading the source it stamps.

summary: A malformed or non-mapping YAML frontmatter block becomes an error finding naming the file — parse_frontmatter silently returns an empty field map today, so the gate judges fabricated absence

edit.description: parse_frontmatter (lines 265-282) maps a YAML parse failure and a present-but-non-mapping block both to an empty JsonMap. Surface each as an error through the one diagnostic source instead (invariant 6: a failure temper can detect is an error message — no path silently degrades). A deliberate forgiveness must be a declared leniency (representation.md, format: "declaring the leniency its source-reads forgive"), never a silent default.
resolve_kind_units (~lines 807-819) threads the parse failure into check's findings rather than composing over fabricated-empty fields.

tests.asserts: a source with a malformed frontmatter block fails check with an error naming the file and the malformation, never a missing-field finding over silently-empty fields

acceptance: check on a harness containing a malformed frontmatter block reports the malformation as an error naming the file; no path treats it as frontmatter-less silently.
notes: Scoped at f600965. The 0013 note's independently-surviving slice (observed 9c3b1c1, amended 6a04322). Spans and the layout reader derive from 0019 in the spec-delta job — this is the minimal invariant-6 fix; job 2 may extend it. If the harness's documented behavior makes a non-mapping block a real leniency, declare it cited — open question, never invent. Serialized behind REQUIREMENT-PROSE-PERSISTS (shared src/main.rs).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at a641e03 (ship audit; sole intervening src/tests/sdk commit since 6d6ae89 is a620938/REQUIREMENT-KIND-SDK-TYPE, an SDK-only Requirement.kind retype unrelated to packaging): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
